item.title: 👨🏻‍💻 Book Recommendation v2.0
item.description: Introduction As a senior thesis, I did built a book recommendation systems (which I versioned it as v1.0). While it returned a satisfying result, I remember that we had to run the very performant desktop for days to build the utility matrix to get a prediction. Optimization using algorithm and numpy library helped but still took days. In 2019, I got to know more about Spark/Scala and its power. So I decided to use that instead and check the difference.

item.title: 🏖 Summer vacation in Scandinavian countries
item.description: This summer, I spent a week in Copenhagen, Denmark and 4 cities in west Norway. I’d like to share some thoughts & photos. 🇩🇰 Denmark Copenhagen It was interesting to see so many bicycles all over the city. It makes sense because there’s practically no hill or mountain in Copenhagen. But they also have very organized road systems to encourage people to use them, which is great. The city itself was very clean as well with no rats running in the metro or dead cockroaches on the streets.

item.title: 👨🏻‍💻 Book Recommendation v1.0
item.description: Introduction There are 3 major approaches for recommendation systems: content-based, collaborative, and hybrid. Content-based: Recommend items to a user that are similar to the ones that user preferred in the past. For example, if Nancy likes <28 Days Later> 🧟‍♂️, we analyze the contents of the this movie like genre, country, year etc. After that analysis, we can deduce that Nancy will probably like <Train to Busan> as well.

item.title: 👨🏻‍💻 Radiation Map of the World v1.0
item.description: Motivation Curiosity - As a Korean who was born after 1990, Fukushima Daiichi accident is by far one of the most suspicious and concerning nuclear accidents. This just happened in 2011 in Fukushima Prefecture, 220km away from Tokyo. (That is about the distance between DC and Philadelphia!) I heard that this accident was as severe (or worse) as Chernobyl. However, strangely no one really knows what really happened and about 30 million tourists still go to Japan.

item.title: 🌿 Don't look back
item.description: I often look back what I’ve done. It usually involves pretty bad regret and self-degradation. It can be about my career, relationship or my whole life. These regrets slowly transform how I perceive the world or behave. Like Chihiro forgot her name, this kind of self-reflection is destructive. Re-watching an animation I watched when I was 9 was quite deeply enlightening. It was 17 years ago but I pretty clearly remember the smell of my friend’s house, smile of my friend’s mom and pure love I had to people and life.

item.title: 🌿 Joy of Reading and Sharing
item.description: I would like to share books I’ve been reading and things I learned or made in this blog. Thanks!

item.title: About
item.description: Hello. 👋 This is Jinny Cho. I studied Computer Science in Carleton College and currently work as a software engineer. Have been massaging maps api to be more performant, scalable and maintainable. Also started to work on large data processing and pipelining. I’m also personally intersted in learning more about computer graphics, data visualization and app development. Outside of technology, I’m interested in writing, drawing and living a fuller and more peaceful life.
